zh.lifeSummary: 當Frances Dennis於1560年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾普可萊其時，她的父親Hugh Dennis35歲，母親Katherine Trye35歲。她於1584年與Thomas Lygon在英國武斯特斯爾馬德雷斯費爾德結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1624年1月30日在英國維爾爾享年64歲，埋葬在英國瓦維其可爾索維。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Frances Dennis于1560年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾普可萊其时，她的父亲Hugh Dennis35岁，母亲Katherine Trye35岁。她于1584年与Thomas Lygon在英国武斯特斯爾馬德雷斯費爾德结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1624年1月30日在英国維爾爾享年64岁，埋葬在英国瓦維其可爾索維。

nameMeanings.name: Dennis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Denis(e), Dinis(e), vernacular forms of Latin Dionysius, Greek Dionysios ‘(follower) of Dionysos’, an eastern god introduced to the classical pantheon at a relatively late date and bearing a name of probably Semitic origin. The name was borne by various early Christian saints, including Saint Denis, the martyred 3rd-century bishop of Paris who became the patron saint of France. From the 12th century onward, the popularity of the name in England (and of its cognates, mainly Denis , in the continental Europe) seems to have been largely due to French influence. The feminine form Dionysia (in vernacular English likewise Den(n)is) is also found, and some examples of the surname may originate from it.
English: from Middle English deneis, a variant of danais ‘Danish’, with the vowel influenced by Middle English denshe (Old English denisc). Compare Dence .

Irish (mainly Dublin and Cork): of the same origin as 1 and 2 above, sometimes an alternative form to Donohue but more often to McDonough , since the personal name Donnchadh was Anglicized as Donough or Denis.

variants.name: Dec
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Aleksander, Andrzej, Casimir, Ignacy, Jerzy, Krystyna, Krzysztof, Malgorzata, Mikolaj, Stanislaw, Tadeusz.
Polish: from a pet form of the personal name Dionizy, Polish form of Dionysius (see Dennis ).

variants.name: Dennison
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Denis (see Dennis ) + -son. Compare Tennyson .
English (of Norman origin): from Anglo-Norman French deinzein, a burgess who enjoyed the privileges of those living deinz la cité ‘within the city’.

variants.name: Denny
meanings: English and Scottish: from Middle English Den(n)y, a pet form of the personal name Denis (see Dennis ). Compare Tenney .

English: habitational name from Danny in Hurstpierpoint, Sussex, recorded as Danye in 1343. Alternatively, the name may arise from Denny in Waterbeach, Cambridgeshire, but no medieval evidence with a habitative preposition has been found in the case of the latter.
Scottish: habitational name from Denny in Stirlingshire.

variants.name: Diniz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Augusto, Celso, Francisco, Luis, Ricardo, Rogerio, Alvaro, Anselmo, Antero, Carlos.  Portuguese Joao, Marcio.
Portuguese: from the personal name Dinis, a vernacular form of Dionysius (see Dennis ).

variants.name: Denes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Bela, Laszlo, Miklos, Tibor, Aranka, Balint, Endre, Ferenc, Lajos, Zoltan.  Jewish Imrich, Meyer.  Romanian Doina, Matei, Radu.
Hungarian (Dénes): from the personal name Dénes, from Greek Dionysios (see Dennis ). The surname Dénes is also found in Slovakia; compare 2 below.
Slovak (Deneš and Déneš) and Romanian (also Deneş): from the Hungarian personal name Dénes ‘Dennis’, or an adaptation of the Hungarian surname Dénes (see 1 above).

variants.name: Dennett
meanings: English: from a pet form of Middle English Denis (see Dennis ), or possibly from a pet form of Denise, as, for example, Denote, a female character in Piers Plowman.

variants.name: Dinges
meanings: German: from a vernacular form of the Latin personal name Dionysius (see Dennis ). Compare Dings .

variants.name: Denis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Jacques, Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Serge, Yvan, Alphonse, Edouard, Francois, Gaetane.  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Roberto, Angel, Francisco, Joaquin, Juan, Manuel, Miguel, Ramon, Rolando.

French, Walloon, Breton, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, Dutch, English, and Portuguese; Spanish and Galician (Denís): from the personal name Denis, Spanish and Galician Denís, from Latin Dionysius ‘(follower) of Dionysos’ (see Dennis ). Compare Dennee .
Ukrainian and Rusyn: variant transliteration of   Denys  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Dings
meanings: Dutch and German: from a vernacular form of the Latin personal name Dionysius (see Dennis ). Compare Dinges .

variants.name: Dinis
meanings: Portuguese and Galician: from Dinis, a Portuguese personal name equivalent to Dennis .

nameMeanings.name: Frances
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Feminine form of   Francis .  In the 16th century the two spellings were used indiscriminately for both sexes, the distinction in spelling not being established until the 17th century.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Frances Dennis was born in 1560, in Pucklechurch, Gloucestershire, England, her father, Hugh Dennis, was 35 and her mother, Katherine Trye, was 35. She married Thomas Lygon in 1584, in Madresfield, Worcestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 30 January 1624, in Wiltshire, England, at the age of 64, and was buried in Sowe, Warwickshire, England.

Elle est décédée le 30 janvier 1624, à Wiltshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Sowe, Warwickshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Frances Dennisが1560年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアプウックレチャーチで生まれた時，父Hugh Dennisは35歳，母Katherine Tryeは35歳であった。1584年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアマドレスフィールドでThomas Lygonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1624年1月30日にイギリスウィルトシュアイアで64歳で死去し，イギリスワーウィクシュアイアソウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Frances Dennis(이)가 1560년 영국 그루스스터실르 푸클렉후르치에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugh Dennis(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Katherine Trye(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 1584년 영국 워스스터실르 매드레스필드에서 Thomas Lygon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1624년 1월 30일 영국 윌츠히르에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워윅실르 소우에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Thomas Lygon em 1584, em Madresfield, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 5 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Frances Dennis родилась в 1560 г. в местности пукклечурч, глоусестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Hugh Dennis было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Katherine Trye, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Lygon в 1584 г. в местности мадресфиелд, ворсестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 30 января 1624 г. в местности уилцхир, Англия в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности соу, варвиккшир, Англия.
